Oracle Events
OE1: Search the CRM for all deals associated with Catalina's accounts including Canopy Health, NorthWind Technologies, PivotPoint Labs, Prairie Data Consulting, and Lakeline Logistics using crm_search_deals (separate calls per company or by company_id). This reveals all active and closed deals, the duplicate NorthWind Denver entries, two Lakeline deals (deal_lakeline still sitting at proposalMade alongside deal_lakeline_lost already at closedLost — the original record was never updated), the NorthWind Q1 deal still at proposalMade instead of closedWon, and the Dr. Anand Mehta executive relocation at $12,170 (managed by Mina Hashimoto, not Catalina).
OE2: Search QuickBooks for invoice data using quickbooks_search_invoices to check billing status across all accounts. This reveals that the NorthWind Q1 invoice INV-2026-003 for $60,000 is overdue since February 19, the Prairie Data invoice INV-2026-004 for $4,800 is overdue since February 21, and the Canopy invoice INV-2026-0401 for $9,210 includes $2,650 in unapproved surcharges.
OE3: Search emails for Patricia's response to the surcharge approval request using search_emails with queries like "Patricia surcharge" or "INV-2026-0401". This confirms Patricia has not responded to the approval request sent on April 2.
OE4: Search Slack messages for the Heartland billing situation and Mosaic overlap using conversations_search_messages. This reveals the Heartland Q1 bill of $12,800 is on hold, the Mosaic $90K damage claim with a $40K uncovered gap, and the cross-account vendor dependency with Emeka's accounts (GreenStack's Priya Venkatesh was incorrectly billed by Heartland when Swift handled her move).
OE5: Search emails and Slack for Prairie Data resolution status using search_emails and conversations_search_messages. This confirms the deal is closedWon and the relocation was completed, but cross-referencing with QuickBooks from OE2 reveals the $4,800 invoice is still unpaid.
OE6: Search Slack for coordinator assignments and workload using conversations_search_messages and conversations_history on the operations channel, and cross-reference with Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records. This reveals Blessing Okafor is carrying 5 concurrent active moves (Marcus Webb/Canopy, Kevin Okafor/Canopy, Preethi Nair/BrightLoop, Raj Patel/NorthWind, Emilia Cruz/NorthWind), Fatimah Al-Rashidi has 6 active moves (Dr. Renata Kovacs/Canopy, Tomás Herrera/GreenStack, Lena Kowalski/Mosaic, Mei-Lin Zhao/GreenStack, Nadia Okonjo/PivotPoint, plus the Dr. Mehta executive relocation), and GreenStack April coordinators are assigned (Mei-Lin Zhao to Fatimah, Joaquín Reyes to Javier Morales). Both Blessing and Fatimah are at or above capacity, with Fatimah's load being the highest resource risk to flag to David.
OE7: Search emails and Slack for NorthWind Denver expansion progress using search_emails and conversations_search_messages. This reveals the chain-of-custody protocol is unprecedented for MoveOps and no template exists yet (Chloe confirmed in #operations she does not have a custody document template), Victor Huang sent a formal 5-point security requirements document for the server lab move, Gentle Giant Piano Movers is a potential subcontractor for the Steinway but has no existing vendor agreement with MoveOps, and Catalina internally framed the Denver expansion as a second chance after NorthWind's Q1 satisfaction score of 5/10.
OE8: Search the CRM for all Canopy Health deals using crm_search_deals with query "Canopy". This returns five active deals including the Dr. Anand Mehta executive relocation at $12,170 which is Canopy's highest-value individual deal and represents the first C-suite placement for Canopy Health. The Mehta deal is managed by Mina Hashimoto, not Catalina.
OE9: Look up contact details for email recipients using contacts_search_contacts for David Chen, Emeka Diallo, Chloe Vance, and Patricia Langford. For Pam Kowalski, use crm_search_leads (query: "Pam Kowalski") or locate her email from existing email threads, as she is not in the contacts service. Her email is pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com (found in CRM leads as lead_northwind).
OE10: Send an email to David Chen at david.chen@moveops.com with the full portfolio summary covering pipeline numbers, deal statuses across all accounts, billing flags including overdue invoices and unapproved surcharges, and key risks for the Friday one on one.
OE11: Send an email to Patricia Langford at patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io with a heads-up on the $2,650 in unapproved Canopy surcharges that are blocking vendor commitments across the three active relocations.
OE12: Send an email to Pam Kowalski at pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com with an update on where the Denver expansion relocations stand based on investigation findings.
OE13: Send an email to Emeka Diallo at emeka.diallo@moveops.com briefing him on the Heartland billing situation, the Mosaic damage claim, and any GreenStack overlap that touches his accounts.
OE14: Send an email to Chloe Vance at chloe.vance@moveops.com requesting a coordinator capacity check and flagging any resource risks across Catalina's active moves, particularly Fatimah's 6 concurrent assignments.
OE15: Update the Lakeline Logistics deal in the CRM from proposalMade to closedLost using crm_update_deal_stage since the deal was lost to a competitor.
OE16: Update the NorthWind Q1 deal in the CRM from proposalMade to closedWon using crm_update_deal_stage since the five relocations were completed and invoiced.
OE17: Update the duplicate NorthWind Denver expansion deal to align its stage with the primary deal record using crm_update_deal_stage.
OE18: Add CRM engagement notes on each client account using crm_create_engagement for NorthWind, Canopy Health, PivotPoint Labs, and Prairie Data Consulting (and optionally Mosaic and GreenStack), documenting the current status, risks, and actions taken.
OE19: Post a portfolio recap in the customer engagement Slack channel using conversations_add_message so the rest of the CE team has visibility into Catalina's book of business.
